reasoning: The resolution criteria requires two consecutive quarters of negative GDP (advance estimates) entirely within 2025. Q1 recorded -0.5%, but Q2 recorded a strongly positive advance estimate of +3.0%. Because Q2 was positive, there was no Q1-Q2 or Q2-Q3 recession. The only remaining path to a YES resolution is if both Q3 and Q4 advance estimates were negative. Expected Q3 2025 estimates in late October projected growth between +1.3% and +3.8% (Atlanta Fed), and the market implied probability traded down to 2% soon after the Q3 advance estimate would have been released. With Q3 GDP effectively confirmed as positive, it is mathematically impossible to have two consecutive negative quarters in 2025, bringing the true probability to near 0. I am submitting the minimum allowed probability of 0.02.
